heading: Medical Department: composition
label: 8219 Medical Department: composition
text: The Medical Corps and Dental Corps, and such other staff corps as the Secretary of the Navy may establish under and designate to be in the Medical Department of the Navy, are in the Medical Department of the Navy.section 8090(b) of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)6027(1st 20 words of 1st sentence).34 U.S.C. 30a(1st 20 words of 1st sentence), .Aug. 4, 1947, ch. 459, § 20161 Stat. 736(less 2d sentence).34 U.S.C. 43(less 2d sentence), ; , .Apr. 16, 1947, ch. 38, § 20161 Stat. 47Aug. 7, 1947, ch. 512, § 434(a)61 Stat. 882.34 U.S.C. 32, (30th through 44th words of 6th par. under “Hospital Corps”).Aug. 29, 1916, ch. 41739 Stat. 573(26th through 37th words).34 U.S.C. 51(1st par. under “Naval Dental Corps”, 75th word to end of 1st sentence); added , (4th par.).Aug. 29, 1916, ch. 417July 1, 1918, ch. 11440 Stat. 708There is no provision of law specifically stating that the Medical Corps is in the Medical Department. It was the first corps to have duties relating to medical and sanitary matters and so long as it was the only corps having such duties there was no need for the departmental concept. The subsequent establishment of other corps with related duties “in the Medical Department” indicates clearly that the Medical Corps is in that Department.The words “effective ” and the words “establishing the Medical Service Corps” in are omitted as executed. The words “is created and established as a Staff Corps of the United States Navy” in are omitted as executed.August 4, 194734 U.S.C. 30a34 U.S.C. 43

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 8219, added , , , related to authorized strength of Air Force in reserve commissioned officers in grades below brigadier general in active status, prior to repeal by , , . Amendments2018— renumbered as this section and substituted “section 8090(b)” for “section 5150(b)”.Pub. L. 115–232section 6027 of this title1986— substituted “section 5150(b)” for “section 5155(b)”.Pub. L. 99–4331980— authorized the Secretary of the Navy to designate staff corps as being in the Medical Department of the Navy and deleted specific references to the Medical Service Corps, the Nurse Corps, and the Hospital Corps as being in such Medical Department.Pub. L. 96–513
